note: Reducer synthesis was not emitted by the LEAF; this registry was reconciled by hand from the four iteration records (P0=0, P1=1, P2=2) and each finding confirmed against the cited file:line before resolution. A repo-wide scan (190 box-containing files, 0 relying on '└─' alone) proved the detector fix regression-free.

findings.title: Connector validator counted '└─' box borders as connectors, so disconnected box diagrams passed the gate the contract promised
findings.evidence: check_arrows counted '→|↓|├─|└─'; '└─' is also a normal bottom-left box border (simple_workflow.md:33,37), so a box-only diagram satisfied 'boxes with no connectors is an error' (SKILL.md:227) via its borders.
findings.resolution: Removed '└─' from the arrow detector and added the recommended '▼|▶' glyphs -> detector is now '→|↓|▼|▶|├─'. Verified: all 6 packet assets still pass the connector check (via ▼); a border-only box fixture now correctly errors; repo-wide scan found 0 flowcharts relying on '└─' alone, so zero regression. SKILL.md:227 is now an accurate contract.

findings.title: SKILL.md-recommended arrow glyphs were not recognized by the validator's connector detector
findings.evidence: SKILL.md:114-116 recommends '▼' (top-to-bottom) and '──▶' (horizontal), but validate_flowchart.sh:63 counted only '→|↓|├─|└─'; every packet asset uses '▼' as its primary vertical connector yet the validator recognized none of it (4 of the 6 assets passed HEAD's connector gate solely via miscounted '└─' borders; the other 2 also contained real '→' arrows).
findings.resolution: Added '▼|▶' to the arrow detector (same one-line change as P1-001). The recommended-and-used glyphs are now first-class connectors; validator and contract notation are aligned. Purely additive for arrow recognition (a diagram can only move from fail to pass), no regression.

findings.title: Top-level README duplicated the SKILL.md primary numbered workflow instead of staying route-map oriented
findings.evidence: README §4 QUICK START restated the five-step authoring workflow already owned by SKILL.md, increasing drift risk for the dissected route-map structure.
findings.resolution: Shrunk README QUICK START to a route-map pointer (a minimal validate example plus 'use SKILL.md for the full workflow and references/README.md for overflow'). README re-validated 0 issues.

outOfScopeNoted: validate_flowchart.sh flags 'Too many box width variations' (rc=1) on 5 of 6 packet example assets; confirmed pre-existing (identical rc on git HEAD's script) and unrelated to any of the three findings — left for a separate box-width normalization pass.
